channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: China Imports 1.4 Million Barrels of Iranian Oil Daily
item.description: Analysts estimate that China imports approximately 1.4 million barrels of oil per day from Iran, representing about 12% of its total oil imports. This substantial volume underscores China's role as th...

item.title: New Linux Local Privilege Escalation Vulnerability 'Fragnesia' Disclosed with Patch Pending
item.description: A new Linux vulnerability named Fragnesia has been publicly disclosed, following in the footsteps of previous flaws like Dirty Frag. The bug allows local attackers to perform arbitrary byte writes int...

item.title: Early Career Highlights and Portraits of Shannen Doherty in the 1980s
item.description: Shannen Doherty (April 12, 1971 – July 13, 2024) began her acting career as a child in the early 1980s, quickly becoming a recognizable talent in television and film. At age 10, she made her debut on ...

item.title: Prince Harry voices concern over rise in antisemitic incidents across the UK
item.description: The Duke of Sussex, Prince Harry, has expressed significant concern over a noticeable increase in antisemitic incidents in the United Kingdom. He highlighted that such a rise is deeply troubling for s...

item.title: Flooding at Heathrow Airport leaves several parked cars partially submerged
item.description: A section of a car park and an adjacent road at London Heathrow Airport was recently flooded due to a localised water issue, affecting numerous vehicles. Footage circulating on social media showed sev...

item.title: James Carville Comments on Trump's Cognitive Decline and Behavior
item.description: During a recent episode of the Politicon podcast, Democratic strategist James Carville discussed former President Donald Trump's apparent cognitive decline. Carville responded to comments by host Al H...

item.title: Cooking Gas Prices Hit ₦1,500 per kg Amid Supply Shortage Across Nigeria
item.description: For Nigeria, domestic cooking gas (LPG) don rise sharply, with 12.5kg cylinder now selling for ₦18,750, up from ₦16,250 last month. The price per kilogram don increase to ₦1,500 from ₦1,300, making en...

item.title: Neighbour describes man shot by police during stand-off as polite and non-threatening
item.description: Neighbours have described a 41-year-old man shot dead by Bedfordshire police during an armed stand-off as polite, harmless, and generally well-behaved, despite recent concerning behaviour. Police atte...

item.title: Senator Bato dela Rosa remains in Senate after shooting incident as investigation continues
item.description: Senator Ronald 'Bato' Dela Rosa remained inside the Senate building following a shooting incident on Wednesday night, according to the Philippine National Police (PNP). PNP spokesperson Police Brigadi...

item.title: Punjab Incorporates Marka-i-Haq in Curriculum and Cultural Programmes
item.description: Punjab government has announced that the four-day conflict between Pakistan and India, known as Marka-i-Haq, will be included in the academic curriculum to instil patriotism among students. The decisi...

item.title: US waives visa bonds for World Cup fans and team members
item.description: Di United States don announce say dem go relax some of di visa requirements wey dey for people wey wan travel go World Cup. Normally, Trump administration dey ask citizens from about 50 developing cou...

item.title: Police Investigate Deaths of Three Young Women Found in Brighton Sea
item.description: Police in Brighton are investigating the deaths of three young women whose bodies were discovered in the sea near Madeira Drive. Officers were alerted early on Wednesday morning following concerns for...

item.title: How the Milk Carton Campaign Raised Awareness for Missing Children in 1980s America
item.description: During the 1980s, the United States launched a distinctive public awareness initiative known as the Missing Children Milk Carton Campaign. This program printed photos and basic information of missing ...

item.title: RSS Leader and Former Indian Army Chief Advocate Continued Dialogue with Pakistan
item.description: A senior leader of the Rashtriya Swayamsevak Sangh (RSS), Dattatreya Hosabale, along with former Indian Army Chief Manoj Mukund Naravane, have publicly expressed support for keeping diplomatic channel...

item.title: The Popularity of Roman Sandals in 1960s and 1970s Fashion
item.description: Roman sandals, also known as gladiator sandals, became a defining fashion trend during the late 1960s and early 1970s. Characterized by multiple thin leather straps that wrapped around the foot, ankle...

item.title: Exploring the Mesmerizing Kinetic Typography by Syddharth Mate
item.description: Syddharth Mate, a talented designer, contributes to the ongoing 36 Days of Type project with his captivating kinetic typography series. His work stands out for its innovative use of negative space and...

item.title: Marco Rubio Wears Casual Outfit Resembling Nicolás Maduro During Travel to China
item.description: On May 12, 2026, Secretary of State Marco Rubio was photographed wearing a gray Nike tracksuit while traveling to China on Air Force One. The outfit resembled the attire former Venezuelan President Ni...

item.title: Trump Administration Criticizes Judge After Failing to Disclose Important Immigration Case Details
item.description: The Trump administration is escalating its conflict with a federal judge in Rhode Island, despite being responsible for a procedural oversight in an immigration case. Judge Melissa R. DuBose had prepa...

item.title: Africa-France Summit 2026 Commits to Stronger Agriculture and Health Partnerships
item.description: During the Africa Forward Summit held in Nairobi, African and French leaders adopted a declaration that sets out strong commitments to transform agriculture and health systems across the continent. Th...

item.title: South Africa Gains from China's Temporary Zero-Tariff Scheme for African Exports
item.description: The South African Minister of Trade, Industry and Competition, Parks Tau, has welcomed China's announcement of a temporary zero-tariff preference scheme for 20 African countries, including South Afric...
